title: dryopteris laxa
intro: Dryopteris laxa, also known as Western wood fern or brittle wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family and is native to western North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris laxa include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as being used as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Dryopteris laxa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
